nameMeanings.name: DeLaval
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: variant of Delavant (and, in North America, an altered form of this), itself probably a variant of Delavaud, a habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone from Lavaud, the name of several places in the westcentral part of France. This form of the surname has apparently died out in France.
History: Cornelius Del Avant/Delavan of French descent married Deborah Green in Stamford, CT, in 1712.

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs. Gilbert DeLaval was born in 1173, in Worcester, Worcestershire, England. She married Gilbert Delaval about 1193, in Chaddesley Corbett, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 1 daughter.

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs. Gilbert DeLaval(은)는 1173년 영국 워스스터실르 워스스터에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1193년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 채데슬리 코벳에서 Gilbert Delaval(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.
